action.reasoning: Observation: the attempted improvement failed immediately because train.py tried to download ResNet18 weights, but network is disabled. Analysis: this is a straightforward offline-loading issue, not a modeling failure. We already have a strong valid checkpoint at LPIPS 0.3922, so the safest next step is to remove all network-dependent code, use only local/offline losses, train a somewhat stronger model longer, and then evaluate. Decision: patch train.py to use LPIPS(vgg) and pixel losses only (cached locally per task description), keep predict.py unchanged/backward-compatible, train a larger 128px model for more epochs, then submit the new checkpoint if training succeeds.
